name: Smartcar Connected Vehicle API
what_it_does: Connected vehicle platform REST API providing standardized access to vehicle data (location, odometer, fuel/battery level, lock/unlock) across 36+ car brands through a single API with user consent.
use_cases: Querying vehicle location and odometer for fleet management and insurance agents
Reading EV battery level and charging status for range optimization agents
Remotely locking/unlocking vehicles via API for car-sharing applications
Aggregating vehicle health data across multiple brands from a single integration
Building UBI (usage-based insurance) applications using real-time odometer data
not_for: Real-time vehicle diagnostics requiring OBD-II adapter hardware
Commercial fleet management at scale (use Samsara or Geotab)
Non-consumer vehicles without factory-installed connectivity (older vehicles pre-2015)
best_when: An agent needs standardized vehicle data access across multiple car brands via user-consented OAuth2 without brand-specific API integrations.
avoid_when: You need commercial/industrial vehicle fleet management — Smartcar targets consumer/prosumer vehicles.

auth.notes: OAuth2 for vehicle owner consent. Per-vehicle access tokens after user authorization. Granular scopes per vehicle capability (read_odometer, read_location, control_security). User consent required for each vehicle.

pricing.paid_tiers: From $0.05/API call; Starter plan from $50/month; Scale from $500/month
pricing.notes: Pay-per-call pricing for API calls. Connected vehicle count based pricing for larger fleets. Transparent pricing calculator. Enterprise volume discounts available.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Well-documented REST API with SDK support. Error responses are JSON with vehicle-specific error codes. Webhook delivery for vehicle events. API compatibility matrix per brand documented.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Read operations are idempotent. Vehicle control commands (lock/unlock) are not idempotent by nature but safe to retry.
known_agent_gotchas: Vehicle connectivity varies — car must have cellular signal and be on for some operations
OEM API limitations: not all brands support all endpoints (e.g., some don't expose location)
User OAuth consent required per vehicle — agents can't access vehicles without owner approval
API latency varies by OEM server response time — can be 2-10 seconds for vehicle commands

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 Type II. GDPR compliant with EU data residency. OAuth2 with granular consent scopes per vehicle capability. User consent model ensures vehicle owner controls data access. TLS enforced.
